message:
Add canonical WASM browser profiles and mutual-exclusion compile guards

Introduce four canonical feature profiles (FP-BR-DEV, FP-BR-PROD,
FP-BR-DET, FP-BR-MIN) for wasm32 browser builds and enforce
exactly-one-profile selection at compile time, replacing the previous
single wasm-browser-preview gate.

Cargo.toml:
- Add capability-slice features: wasm-runtime, browser-io,
  browser-trace, deterministic-mode, native-runtime
- Add four canonical profile features: wasm-browser-dev (runtime+io),
  wasm-browser-prod (runtime+io), wasm-browser-deterministic
  (runtime+deterministic+trace), wasm-browser-minimal (runtime only)

src/lib.rs:
- Replace single wasm-browser-preview compile_error with a guard
  requiring exactly one canonical profile on wasm32
- Add mutual-exclusion compile_error for all six pairwise profile
  combinations, preventing ambiguous build configurations
- Add compile_error for native-runtime feature on wasm32
- Add compile_error for browser-io and browser-trace with
  wasm-browser-minimal, enforcing the minimal profile contract
- Re-export new wasm_abi types (WasmAbiVersion, WasmAbiSignature,
  WasmHandleRef, boundary state machine, compatibility classifier,
  and 20+ related types) from the public API

.github/wasm_dependency_policy.json:
- Rename profiles from wasm-core-no-default/wasm-core-default/
  wasm-audit-all-features to FP-BR-MIN/FP-BR-DEV/FP-BR-PROD/FP-BR-DET
  using the canonical feature flags with --no-default-features
- Update waiver notes to reflect browser adapter optionalization

docs/wasm_dependency_audit_policy.md:
- Align profile documentation with FP-BR-* naming
- Update example --only-profile command

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -6,25 +6,32 @@
   },
   "profiles": [
     {
-      "id": "wasm-core-no-default",
+      "id": "FP-BR-MIN",
       "target": "wasm32-unknown-unknown",
       "no_default_features": true,
       "all_features": false,
-      "features": []
+      "features": ["wasm-browser-minimal"]
     },
     {
-      "id": "wasm-core-default",
+      "id": "FP-BR-DEV",
       "target": "wasm32-unknown-unknown",
-      "no_default_features": false,
+      "no_default_features": true,
+      "all_features": false,
+      "features": ["wasm-browser-dev"]
+    },
+    {
+      "id": "FP-BR-PROD",
+      "target": "wasm32-unknown-unknown",
+      "no_default_features": true,
       "all_features": false,
-      "features": []
+      "features": ["wasm-browser-prod"]
     },
     {
-      "id": "wasm-audit-all-features",
+      "id": "FP-BR-DET",
       "target": "wasm32-unknown-unknown",
-      "no_default_features": false,
-      "all_features": true,
-      "features": []
+      "no_default_features": true,
+      "all_features": false,
+      "features": ["wasm-browser-deterministic"]
     }
   ],
   "forbidden_crates": [
@@ -80,7 +87,7 @@
       "owner": "runtime-core",
       "replacement_issue": "asupersync-umelq.3.2",
       "expires_at_utc": "2026-06-30T00:00:00Z",
-      "notes": "Tracked conditional usage while canonical browser feature profiles are finalized."
+      "notes": "Tracked conditional usage while browser adapter optionalization and policy closure continue."
     }
   ],
   "output": {

@@ -35,6 +35,17 @@ default = ["test-internals"]
 # Explicit opt-in for experimental browser-targeted builds.
 # This does not imply full wasm parity; it only enables guarded compilation paths.
 wasm-browser-preview = []
+# Browser runtime capability slices for wasm profile composition.
+wasm-runtime = ["wasm-browser-preview"]
+browser-io = []
+browser-trace = []
+deterministic-mode = []
+native-runtime = []
+# Canonical wasm browser profiles (FP-BR-* in the wasm build plan).
+wasm-browser-dev = ["wasm-runtime", "browser-io"]
+wasm-browser-prod = ["wasm-runtime", "browser-io"]
+wasm-browser-deterministic = ["wasm-runtime", "deterministic-mode", "browser-trace"]
+wasm-browser-minimal = ["wasm-runtime"]
 # Enable internal test helpers for integration tests.
 # This exposes private APIs like Cx::new() and is NOT for production use.
 test-internals = ["dep:tracing", "dep:tracing-subscriber", "dep:visibility"]

@@ -19,11 +19,13 @@ Audit browser-target dependency closure and block merges when forbidden runtime
 
 ## Deterministic Profiles
 
-The policy currently audits three `wasm32-unknown-unknown` profiles:
+The policy audits the canonical `FP-BR-*` browser profiles on
+`wasm32-unknown-unknown`:
 
-- `wasm-core-no-default`
-- `wasm-core-default`
-- `wasm-audit-all-features`
+- `FP-BR-MIN` (`--no-default-features --features wasm-browser-minimal`)
+- `FP-BR-DEV` (`--no-default-features --features wasm-browser-dev`)
+- `FP-BR-PROD` (`--no-default-features --features wasm-browser-prod`)
+- `FP-BR-DET` (`--no-default-features --features wasm-browser-deterministic`)
 
 Each profile executes `cargo tree` with deterministic flags (`--prefix depth --charset ascii`) so output ordering is stable and machine-parseable.
 
@@ -66,5 +68,5 @@ Single-profile debugging:
 ```bash
 python3 scripts/check_wasm_dependency_policy.py \
   --policy .github/wasm_dependency_policy.json \
-  --only-profile wasm-audit-all-features
+  --only-profile FP-BR-DET
 ```

@@ -64,12 +64,53 @@
 #![cfg_attr(test, allow(clippy::large_stack_frames))]
 #![cfg_attr(feature = "simd-intrinsics", feature(portable_simd))]
 
-#[cfg(all(target_arch = "wasm32", not(feature = "wasm-browser-preview")))]
+#[cfg(all(
+    target_arch = "wasm32",
+    not(any(
+        feature = "wasm-browser-dev",
+        feature = "wasm-browser-prod",
+        feature = "wasm-browser-deterministic",
+        feature = "wasm-browser-minimal",
+    ))
+))]
 compile_error!(
-    "wasm32 builds are gated. Enable feature `wasm-browser-preview` for explicit browser preview \
-     builds, and follow docs/wasm_dependency_audit_policy.md for supported profiles."
+    "wasm32 builds require exactly one canonical profile feature: `wasm-browser-dev`, \
+     `wasm-browser-prod`, `wasm-browser-deterministic`, or `wasm-browser-minimal`."
 );
 
+#[cfg(all(
+    target_arch = "wasm32",
+    any(
+        all(feature = "wasm-browser-dev", feature = "wasm-browser-prod"),
+        all(feature = "wasm-browser-dev", feature = "wasm-browser-deterministic"),
+        all(feature = "wasm-browser-dev", feature = "wasm-browser-minimal"),
+        all(feature = "wasm-browser-prod", feature = "wasm-browser-deterministic"),
+        all(feature = "wasm-browser-prod", feature = "wasm-browser-minimal"),
+        all(
+            feature = "wasm-browser-deterministic",
+            feature = "wasm-browser-minimal"
+        ),
+    )
+))]
+compile_error!("wasm32 builds must select exactly one canonical browser profile feature.");
+
+#[cfg(all(target_arch = "wasm32", feature = "native-runtime"))]
+compile_error!("feature `native-runtime` is forbidden on wasm32 browser builds.");
+
+#[cfg(all(
+    target_arch = "wasm32",
+    feature = "wasm-browser-minimal",
+    feature = "browser-io"
+))]
+compile_error!("feature `browser-io` is forbidden with `wasm-browser-minimal`.");
+
+#[cfg(all(
+    target_arch = "wasm32",
+    feature = "wasm-browser-minimal",
+    feature = "browser-trace"
+))]
+compile_error!("feature `browser-trace` is forbidden with `wasm-browser-minimal`.");
+
 #[cfg(all(target_arch = "wasm32", feature = "cli"))]
 compile_error!(
     "feature `cli` is unsupported on wasm32 (requires native filesystem/process surfaces)."
@@ -217,7 +258,15 @@ pub use remote::{
 };
 pub use types::{
     Budget, CancelKind, CancelReason, ObligationId, Outcome, OutcomeError, PanicPayload, Policy,
-    RegionId, Severity, SystemPressure, TaskId, Time, join_outcomes,
+    RegionId, Severity, SystemPressure, TaskId, Time, WASM_ABI_MAJOR_VERSION,
+    WASM_ABI_MINOR_VERSION, WASM_ABI_SIGNATURE_FINGERPRINT_V1, WASM_ABI_SIGNATURES_V1,
+    WasmAbiBoundaryEvent, WasmAbiCancellation, WasmAbiChangeClass, WasmAbiCompatibilityDecision,
+    WasmAbiErrorCode, WasmAbiFailure, WasmAbiOutcomeEnvelope, WasmAbiPayloadShape,
+    WasmAbiRecoverability, WasmAbiSignature, WasmAbiSymbol, WasmAbiValue, WasmAbiVersion,
+    WasmAbiVersionBump, WasmBoundaryState, WasmBoundaryTransitionError, WasmHandleKind,
+    WasmHandleRef, classify_wasm_abi_compatibility, is_valid_wasm_boundary_transition,
+    join_outcomes, required_wasm_abi_bump, validate_wasm_boundary_transition,
+    wasm_abi_signature_fingerprint,
 };
 
 // Re-export proc macros when the proc-macros feature is enabled
